raw_responses.query: "mushroom body" OR "drosophila" associative learning two-step circuit transformer attention 2025

results.title: Connectivity biases generate a learning hierarchy in the Drosophila mushroom body
results.snippet: Drosophila mushroom body resolves capacity-selectivity trade-off through projection-neuron-to-Kenyon-cell connectivity biases — some PN types connect up to 15-fold more frequently than others; KC responses scale with PN connectivity; odors activating >20% KCs drive robust associative memory, <10% poorly learned.

results.title: Neuronal architecture of the mushroom body provides a logic for associative learning
results.snippet: 21 MB output neuron types with segregated dendritic arbors along ~2000 Kenyon cells in 15 compartments; convergence of DAN axons on KC-MBON synapses supports valenced learning.

results.title: The Drosophila Mushroom Body: From Architecture to Algorithm in a Learning Circuit
results.snippet: Architecture-to-algorithm review of the Drosophila mushroom body learning circuit.

results.title: A Programmable Model for Exploring the Functional Logic of the Drosophila Mushroom Body
results.snippet: Programmable computational model of MB function.

results.title: A model of associative learning in the mushroom body
results.snippet: Computational model of MB associative learning.
